memberdef.argsstring: (const int16_t *a, size_t N, const int16_t *coeffs, size_t K)
memberdef.name: fir_q15_scalar

briefdescription.para: Allocate and initialise a fixed-point halfband 2:1 decimator.

parameterdescription.para: FIR branch length (number of non-zero symmetric taps, NOT the full sparse prototype length). Must be >= 1.
Float FIR branch coefficients, length num_taps. Must be symmetric (h[k] = h[num_taps-1-k]). Copied and converted to Q15 with x0.5 scaling.

simplesect.para: Non-NULL on success, NULL on invalid args or allocation failure.

detaileddescription.para: Free all resources. NULL is a no-op.
Zero all delay rings and clear the pending-sample flag.

briefdescription.para: Maximum output samples for a given input length.

detaileddescription.para: Returns 0 to trigger the lazy-alloc path in the Python glue: the output buffer is sized to n_in on first call (always sufficient for 2:1).
Returns num_taps as supplied to hbdecim_q15_create.

memberdef.argsstring: (hbdecim_q15_state_t *r, const int16_t *in, size_t n_in, int16_t *out, size_t max_out)
memberdef.name: hbdecim_q15_execute

briefdescription.para: Decimate a block of interleaved IQ int16 samples by 2.

detaileddescription.para: Input layout: I0 Q0 I1 Q1 ... (2*n_in int16_t values). Output layout: I0 Q0 I1 Q1 ... (2*n_out int16_t values), n_out <= n_in/2.
If n_in is odd the trailing even IQ pair is buffered and consumed on the next call. Output buffer must be allocated for at least 2 * ((n_in + 1) / 2) int16_t elements to be safe.

parameterdescription.para: Number of complex input samples (IQ pairs).
Maximum complex output samples to write.

simplesect.para: Number of complex output samples written.

briefdescription.para: Fixed-point halfband 2:1 decimator — Q15 IQ implementation.

detaileddescription.para: See hbdecim_q15_core.h for the full algorithm description.
